title: 157 Mathematics Thus the two rectangles together represent the natural number 2.
subject: Mathematics
unit: Unit L - cardboard as shown in Fig
activityTitle: 157 Mathematics Thus the two rectangles together represent the natural number 2.
objective:
To divide a mixed fraction by a proper fraction [say,   1 3
4   ÷   1
4   ]
materials:
Paper, colour pen, eraser, pencil, cardboard,
adhesive.
procedure:
1.   Draw two circles of equal
radius on a paper and cut
them out and paste on a
cardboard.
2.   Divide each circle into 4
equal parts as shown in
Fig.1.
3.   Shade one circle completely and 3 equal parts in other circle (Fig.   2).
Fig. 2
observation:
1.   In Fig. 1 each rectangle represents the number _________.
2.   In Fig. 2, both rectangles together represent the number _________.
3.   In Fig. 2 each part in a rectangle represents _________.
4.   In Fig. 2, the number of parts representing   1
4   is _________.
5.   2   ÷   1
4   = _________.
discussion:
This activity is useful in explaining division of a natural number by a
fraction.
158 Laboratory Manual – Elementary Stage
